findings.description: The 'From' field layout is altered - in the PDF, the signature appears inline next to 'Division of Waste Management', but in the HTML, the signature appears as a separate block below 'From: Shawn McKee' and above the division/office lines.
findings.evidence: PDF: Signature is positioned to the right of 'Division of Waste Management / Raleigh Central Office'. HTML: Signature appears as a standalone block between 'From: Shawn McKee' and 'Division of Waste Management'.

findings.description: The footer information (State of North Carolina address, phone) appears at the top of the HTML page 2 rather than at the bottom of page 1 as in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: Footer with address/phone at bottom of page 1. HTML: Same information appears at top of the second rendered section.

findings.description: In Section II oral comments summary, the topic labels (Dam Safety, Excavation, Groundwater Assessments, etc.) lose their underline formatting in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF: Topic labels like 'Dam Safety', 'Excavation', 'Groundwater Assessments' are bold and underlined. HTML: These labels appear as plain text without underline styling.

findings.description: In Section III written public comment summary, topic labels lose their underline formatting in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF: Labels like 'Dam Safety', 'Ash recycling and reuse', 'Beneficial Reuse Areas', etc. are bold and underlined. HTML: These labels appear without underline formatting.

findings.description: In Section III continued on page 5, topic labels lose their underline and bold formatting in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: Labels like 'Private Well Issues', 'Risk Classification', 'Surface Water', 'American Indian Input (Lumbee Tribe)' are bold and underlined. HTML: These appear without underline or bold formatting.

findings.description: Nested <main> elements in the HTML. The document has a wrapping <main> from the template and another <main> inside the content, which is invalid HTML structure but does not affect content fidelity.
findings.evidence: The outer template has <main> and the converted content also opens a <main> tag, creating nested main elements.

findings.description: The entire first page content (memorandum header, To/From/Subject, introductory paragraph, and outline) appears twice in the HTML: once in the <header> section and once in the <main> section. The PDF only presents this content once on page 1.
findings.evidence: The header block contains the full memo info (To: Secretary Donald R. van der Vaart, From: Shawn McKee, etc.) and the introductory paragraph and outline. The <main> section repeats all of this same content again.

findings.description: The Attachment I reference text has a minor discrepancy in the date. The PDF page 2 references 'Attachment I - Public Notice of March 16, 2016 Meeting' but the public meeting was on March 10, 2016. The HTML faithfully reproduces this apparent error from the PDF, but in the Attachments section (page 5), the PDF says 'Public Notice of March 10, 2016 Meeting' and the HTML also correctly reflects this. This is actually a discrepancy in the original PDF itself, not an HTML conversion error.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2: 'Attachment I - Public Notice of March 16, 2016 Meeting'; PDF page 5/Attachments: 'Public Notice of March 10, 2016 Meeting'. HTML preserves both references as they appear in the PDF.
